## Preserved Original Text


## Entry 005 — March 14, 2026


### The Johannesburg Signal Changes the Urgency Frame


**How it surfaced:** While writing the Publishing SOP's Audience Architecture section and noting the 22% Johannesburg audience concentration.


**What it reveals:** Johannesburg practitioners are not navigating AI governance as a reform problem (how to fix existing institutional frameworks) but as a _construction problem_ (how to build governance infrastructure where none exists). The Bainbridge Warning lands differently in these two contexts: in the reform context, it is a diagnostic (here is what is broken). In the construction context, it is a prerequisite (here is why you must build this before you scale the system).


**Why this matters:** This is not a minor audience calibration. It is a fundamentally different framing of the entire argument. The construction context is arguably _more urgent_ and _more actionable_ — and Johannesburg practitioners may be the most receptive ICP in the entire audience geography because they are not defending legacy systems.


**Action required:** Write one Johannesburg-specific frame post — not a different argument, but the same argument in the construction register rather than the reform register. Title candidate: _"You Don't Have a Governance Problem. You Have a Foundation Problem."_ LinkedIn primary. Tag Johannesburg practitioners explicitly.
